county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Calhoun. Formed 1854 from Daviess, Muhlenberg, and Ohio. Western Kentucky. McLean County Historical Society. Green River heritage. Small agricultural county. Livermore river crossing records. FamilySearch microfilm. Check parent counties for pre-1854 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1854 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1854. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: McLean County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1854.

source_profiles.source_name: McLean County Coal Mining and Company Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Western Kentucky coalfield. Company town records, mine payrolls, commissary records, and United Mine Workers union records. Essential for documenting mining families.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Brandenburg. Formed 1823 from Breckinridge and Hardin. Ohio River county in central-western Kentucky. Meade County Historical Society. Fort Knox (partially in Meade County) military records. Brandenburg Civil War river crossing records (Morgan's Raid, 1863). Otter Creek Outdoor Recreation Area. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1823 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1823. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Meade County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1823.

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

Tax lists naming slaveholders and number of enslaved; estate inventories naming enslaved individuals; slave schedules (1850, 1860); manumission records; bills of sale. Kentucky was a slave state that did not secede.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Tax lists and estate records generally well preserved

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Kentucky was a border state — both Union and Confederate service records. Union pension files at NARA; Confederate pension files at KDLA. Compiled Military Service Records for both sides. USCT (United States Colored Troops) records for African American soldiers.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1869 from Bath, Montgomery, Morgan, Powell, Wolfe.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Frenchburg. Formed 1869 from five parent counties (Bath, Montgomery, Morgan, Powell, Wolfe). Eastern Kentucky foothills. Menifee County Historical Society. Red River Gorge area. One of Kentucky's smallest counties. Limited institutional resources. FamilySearch microfilm. Check all five parent counties for pre-1869 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1869 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1869. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Menifee County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1869.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Harrodsburg. One of Kentucky's original counties from Virginia era (1785). Harrodsburg (est. 1774) is the oldest permanent English settlement west of the Alleghenies. Old Fort Harrod State Park. Mercer County Historical Society. Beaumont Inn and historical records. Shaker Village of Pleasant Hill records (1805-1910) — exceptional community records. Pleasant Hill Shaker manuscripts at Western Kentucky University and Filson. Pre-1792 records at LVA. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1785 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1785. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Mercer County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1785.

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

Tax lists naming slaveholders and number of enslaved; estate inventories naming enslaved individuals; slave schedules (1850, 1860); manumission records; bills of sale. Kentucky was a slave state that did not secede.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Tax lists and estate records generally well preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Virginia Land Grants — Mercer County Area
source_profiles.repository: Library of Virginia, Richmond / KDLA, Frankfort
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-statehood Virginia land grants, surveys, and patents for Kentucky District. Virginia military bounty warrants (south of Green River). Kentucky was part of Virginia until 1 June 1792.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well preserved at LVA and KDLA

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax lists enumerate heads of household, land acreage, town lots, horses, cattle, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Essential substitute when other records destroyed.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1860 from Adair, Barren, Cumberland, Green, Monroe.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Edmonton. Formed 1860 from Adair, Barren, Cumberland, Green, and Monroe. South-central Kentucky. Metcalfe County Historical Society. Edmonton community records. Green River heritage. Check five parent counties for pre-1860 records. FamilySearch microfilm. Small farming community.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1860 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1860. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Metcalfe County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1860.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Tompkinsville. Formed 1820 from Barren and Cumberland. South-central Kentucky near Tennessee line. Monroe County Historical Society. Old Mulkey Meetinghouse State Historic Site (1804, Daniel Boone's sister buried here). Tompkinsville Civil War battle (1863) records. Cross-border families common with Jackson County, TN. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1820 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1820. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Monroe County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1820.

Kentucky was a border state — both Union and Confederate service records. Union pension files at NARA; Confederate pension files at KDLA. Compiled Military Service Records for both sides. USCT (United States Colored Troops) records for African American soldiers.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Mount Sterling. Formed 1796 from Clark. Eastern Bluegrass transition zone. Montgomery County Historical Society. Mount Sterling is a regional center with city records. Indian Old Fields heritage (Shawnee). Battle of Mount Sterling (1864) records. Gateway to eastern Kentucky. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1796 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1796. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1796.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax lists enumerate heads of household, land acreage, town lots, horses, cattle, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Essential substitute when other records destroyed.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in West Liberty. 1939 courthouse fire destroyed many records. Formed 1822 from Bath and Floyd. Eastern Kentucky mountains. Morgan County Historical Society. West Liberty is on the Licking River. KDLA has some microfilmed copies of pre-fire records. Daniel Boone National Forest encompasses much of county. FamilySearch microfilm for post-1939 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1939 to present. Some gaps due to 1939 courthouse fire destroyed many records.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1939. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1939.

source_profiles.repository: Southeast KY Community & Technical College / UK Special Collections
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Eastern Kentucky coalfield. Company town records, mine payrolls, commissary records, and United Mine Workers union records. Essential for documenting mining families.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Greenville. Formed 1798 from Christian and Logan. Western Kentucky. Muhlenberg County Historical Society. Named for Revolutionary War General Peter Muhlenberg. Coal mining central to county history — Paradise (John Prine's song) was a coal town. Western KY coalfield company records. Central City and Greenville city records. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1798 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1798. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1798.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Western Kentucky coalfield. Company town records, mine payrolls, commissary records, and United Mine Workers union records. Essential for documenting mining families.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Bardstown. One of Kentucky's original counties from Virginia era (1784). Bardstown is one of Kentucky's oldest towns. Nelson County Historical Society. Bourbon capital of the world — Heaven Hill, Jim Beam, Maker's Mark distillery records document workers. My Old Kentucky Home State Park (Federal Hill). St. Joseph Proto-Cathedral records (1816, first inland Catholic cathedral). Strong Catholic heritage — Trappist Abbey of Gethsemani records. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1784 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1784. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).
